response.justification: While the candidate has solid 20 years of diverse engineering experience and recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure (MCP, agentic workflows), this is fundamentally misaligned with a director-level AI Strategy principal engineer role. The AI expertise is too recent (6 months) and narrow, there's no Master's/PhD in AI, no demonstrated AI products or platforms, no evidence of strategic customer advising, and no clear director-level leadership experience. The current career gap (7 months) compounds concerns about whether this is an active, engaged candidate for such a senior role.
strengths: 20 years of diverse technical experience across web, mobile, desktop, embedded systems, cloud (AWS CDK), and infrastructure (Kubernetes, Docker)
Recent hands-on exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators shows technical fluency with emerging AI tooling
Track record of building and shipping technical products (GISCollective used by Green Map, WatchUp Apple TV in top 10) plus open-source contributions
concerns: AI expertise is extremely recent (6 months at re:cinq, April-October 2025) and insufficient for a principal AI strategy role; no long-term AI/ML expertise demonstrated
No Master's degree or PhD in AI field (preferred qualification); no recognized AI products, papers, or thought leadership in AI domain

Demonstrates hands-on senior engineer background, not director-level strategic leadership; no evidence of advising customers on technology adoption or setting technical vision for disruptive initiatives

key_factors.factor: Missing advanced degree in AI and current career gap
